title: Relative Clauses Mastery Test
description: 1200 original multiple-choice relative clause questions for General English, VSTEP, IELTS, TOEIC, and Cambridge-style practice.
sourceNotes: Original questions generated from relative pronoun, defining/non-defining, zero relative, preposition, reduced clause, and free relative patterns; not copied from source materials.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct relative clause. Focus: who + verb. Context set 0001.
options: The student who joined the class last week is in my class.
The student where joined the class last week is in my class.
The student whose joined the class last week is in my class.
The student which joined the class last week is in my class.
questions.answer: The student who joined the class last week is in my class.
questions.explanation: Use who for people when the relative pronoun is the subject of the relative clause.

questions.prompt: Choose the best relative pronoun or structure. Focus: which + verb. Context set 0002.
options: The book who contains useful examples is on the desk.
The book which contains useful examples is on the desk.
The book whose contains useful examples is on the desk.
The book where contains useful examples is on the desk.
questions.answer: The book which contains useful examples is on the desk.
questions.explanation: Use which for things or animals when the relative pronoun is the subject.

questions.prompt: Which option uses the relative clause correctly? Focus: who + verb. Context set 0003.
options: The candidate where won the speaking prize is in my class.

The candidate who won the speaking prize is in my class.

questions.answer: The candidate who won the speaking prize is in my class.
questions.explanation: Use who for people when the relative pronoun is the subject of the relative clause.

options: The chart in that we found the answer was reliable.
The chart in where we found the answer was reliable.
The chart which in we found the answer was reliable.
The chart in which we found the answer was reliable.
questions.answer: The chart in which we found the answer was reliable.
questions.explanation: Use which after a fronted preposition when referring to things.

questions.prompt: Complete the idea with the correct relative clause. Focus: which refers to the whole clause. Context set 1189.
options: The customer missed the deadline, which surprised the team.
The customer missed the deadline which surprised the team.
The customer missed the deadline, what surprised the team.
The customer missed the deadline, that surprised the team.
questions.answer: The customer missed the deadline, which surprised the team.
questions.explanation: Which can refer to the whole preceding clause in a non-defining relative clause.

questions.prompt: Select the sentence that fits the relative clause rule. Focus: present participle reduction. Context set 1190.
